factors.summary: Cintas is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2021

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, benefiting the company.

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 50.5% for the quarter ended November 30, 2022, compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 43.5% in the same period of the prior fiscal year. The improvement in gross margin from the second quarter of the prior fiscal year was primarily driven by favorable changes in the sales mix as well as improved leverage of fixed costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2021

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly energy costs, which are partially offset by operational improvements.

evidence.text: This change from the same period in the prior fiscal year was primarily due to higher Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment sales volume, as well as increased energy costs and investments in material cost to support increased revenue growth achieved during the three months ended November 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2021

factors.summary: Cintas is successfully attracting and retaining talent, contributing to business success.

evidence.text: New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2021

factors.summary: The company is reducing selling and administrative expenses as a percentage of revenue, contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: Selling and administrative expenses increased $19.4 million, but decreased as a percent of revenue to 31.7%,... primarily due to efficiencies realized in selling and administrative labor expenses.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2021

factors.summary: Capital expenditures increased, indicating investment in growth opportunities.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $146.4 million and $108.6 million for the six months ended November 30, 2022 and 2021, respectively. The increase... due to an investment in the operating segments to support continued market penetration and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Share buybacks have slowed, but dividends have increased, signaling cautious confidence.

evidence.text: The decrease in cash used in financing activities was primarily due to the decrease in share buyback activity and debt payments partially offset by the increase in dividends paid in the six months ended November 30, 2022.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, supporting liquidity and expansion.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $619.1 million for the six months ended November 30, 2022, compared to $593.8 million for the six months ended November 30, 2021. The change... primarily due to an increase in net income and favorable changes in working capital.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing positively, though at a reduced pace compared to the prior year.

evidence.text: Cash paid for acquisitions of businesses was $15.5 million and $45.7 million for the six months ended November 30, 2022 and 2021, respectively.

factors.summary: The company is confident in the current economic environment, expecting continued adequate liquidity.

evidence.text: We believe the Company has sufficient liquidity to operate in the current business environment for at least the next 12 months and the foreseeable future thereafter.

factors.summary: The company faces ordinary course litigation but does not expect material adverse effects.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings... management, the aggregate liability... will not have a material adverse effect...

factors.summary: The company is demonstrating strong organic revenue growth across its operations, with notable acceleration and significant increases in specific segments.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 13.1% to $2,174.9 million for the three months ended November 30, 2022, compared to $1,922.3 million for the three months ended November 30, 2021. The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 12.8%.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2021

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 15.4%
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2021

factors.summary: Margins are improving, with both gross and operating margins expanding due to operational efficiencies, favorable sales mix, and fixed cost leverage. While there are investments in material costs and increases in energy costs, these factors are being offset by overall improvements in efficiency and cost management.

evidence.text: The improvement in gross margin was the result of efficiencies in labor and improved leverage of fixed costs, partially offset by investments in material cost to support increased revenue growth and a 20 basis point increase in energy costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2021

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 50.0% for the six months ended November 30, 2022, which was an increase of 580 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 44.2% in the same period of the prior fiscal year. The change in gross margin... primarily driven by favorable changes in the sales mix as well as improved leverage of fixed costs.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2022 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2021
